# @attestto/mesh

> Open-source, peer-to-peer data layer for sovereign identity. Keeps citizens' identity state available even when government servers go offline. Not a blockchain. Not a file system. A distributed state-sync layer for encrypted identity data.

Part of the [Attestto Open](https://attestto.org) ecosystem.

## What it is

Traditional digital identity depends on servers. If those servers go down — hurricane, cyberattack, policy change — millions of people lose access to their identity.

Attestto Mesh removes that single point of failure. Identity data is encrypted, fragmented across thousands of peers via a gossip protocol, and cryptographically anchored to Solana. No single entity — not Attestto, not a government, not a cloud provider — can shut it down.

**Blind Courier principle:** every node stores encrypted data it mathematically cannot read. Privacy is a cryptographic guarantee, not a policy.

## What gets stored

| Data type | Size | Stored where |
|---|---|---|
| DID Documents | 1–2 KB | Mesh |
| Verifiable Credentials | 1–5 KB | Mesh |
| Secure Messages (DIDComm) | < 2 KB | Mesh (deleted on ack) |
| Audit Receipts | < 500 B | Mesh (permanent) |
| Private keys | < 1 KB | Device only — never leaves |
| User preferences | 200 B | Device only |
| Files (PDFs, videos) | large | Not stored — this is not a file system |

**Total mesh footprint per citizen: ~120 KB.** A national mesh for 5 million people fits in ~600 GB distributed across thousands of nodes.

## Three-layer resolution

```
1. Local cache    — 0ms      (data already on your device)
2. Mesh peers     — <100ms   (DHT lookup, nearby nodes serve encrypted blobs)
3. Solana anchor  — 200–500ms (cryptographic proof of existence and ordering)
```

If any layer fails, the others keep working. Graceful degradation by design.

## Quick start

```bash
npm install @attestto/mesh
```

```typescript
import { MeshNode, MeshStore, MeshProtocol } from '@attestto/mesh'

// Initialize local storage (default 250 MB)
const store = new MeshStore('/path/to/mesh/data')

// Start a P2P node
const node = new MeshNode({
  dataDir: '/path/to/mesh/data',
  bootstrapPeers: ['/ip4/203.0.113.1/tcp/4001/p2p/Qm...'],
  listenPort: 4001,
})
await node.start()

// Wire up the protocol layer
const protocol = new MeshProtocol(node, store)

// Publish encrypted data to the mesh
const contentHash = await protocol.put({
  didOwner: 'did:sns:maria.sol',
  path: 'credentials/drivers-license',
  version: 1,
  ttlSeconds: 0,           // 0 = permanent
  signature: '...',        // Ed25519 signature of payload by owner
  encryptedPayload: '...',  // AES-256-GCM blob — nodes never see plaintext
})

// Retrieve
const blob = await protocol.get({ didOwner: 'did:sns:maria.sol', path: 'credentials/drivers-license' })
```

## Architecture components

| Component | Purpose | Technology |
|---|---|---|
| `MeshNode` | P2P networking, peer discovery, gossip | libp2p, Kademlia DHT, GossipSub |
| `MeshStore` | Local encrypted blob storage with index | SQLite + flat `.enc` files |
| `MeshProtocol` | PUT/GET/UPDATE/TOMBSTONE operations | Content-addressed, signature-verified |
| `conflict.ts` | Deterministic version arbitration | Solana anchor > version > timestamp > hash |
| `gc.ts` | Automatic cleanup with safety rails | TTL expiry, version pruning, LRU with 6-holder minimum |
| `anchor.ts` | Immutable proof-of-existence | Solana Memo transactions (~$0.00025 each) |
| `daemon.ts` | Background node process | Long-running service for desktop/server |

## Protocol operations

```
PUT     didOwner + path + version + signature + encryptedPayload
          → stores blob, gossips to peers, returns contentHash

GET     didOwner + path
          → tries local cache → mesh DHT → returns latest version blob

UPDATE  didOwner + path + newVersion + signature + encryptedPayload
          → conflict resolution via ordering rules, gossips update

TOMBSTONE didOwner + path + signature
          → marks deleted, propagates revocation in <500ms, GC eventually removes
```

## Conflict resolution ordering

When two nodes have conflicting versions:
1. Solana anchor timestamp (highest authority)
2. Version number
3. Timestamp
4. Hash (deterministic tiebreaker)

No central coordinator needed.

## Country mesh configuration

Each national mesh runs independently. Configure `bootstrapPeers` and `allowlist` to form a country-scoped network:

```typescript
const node = new MeshNode({
  dataDir: '/var/mesh/cr',
  bootstrapPeers: CR_BOOTSTRAP_PEERS,   // Costa Rica mesh entry points
  listenPort: 4001,
  maxStorageMB: 250,
  countryCode: 'cr',
})
```

`attestto-desktop` auto-detects country via system timezone and joins the correct national mesh.

## Source layout

```
src/
  index.ts     — public exports
  node.ts      — MeshNode (libp2p wrapper)
  store.ts     — MeshStore (SQLite + .enc files)
  protocol.ts  — PUT/GET/UPDATE/TOMBSTONE
  anchor.ts    — Solana Memo anchoring
  conflict.ts  — version conflict resolution
  gc.ts        — garbage collection
  crypto.ts    — encryption helpers
  rpc.ts       — local RPC for IPC with desktop app
  daemon.ts    — background daemon process
  types.ts     — TypeScript interfaces
```

## Standards

- W3C DID Core 1.0 — DID Document storage and resolution
- W3C VC Data Model v2.0 — credential storage format
- DIDComm v2 — encrypted message transport
- libp2p — P2P networking (Kademlia DHT, GossipSub)

## Related repos

| Repo | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `attestto-desktop` | Desktop app — each install is a mesh node (250 MB contribution) |
| `attestto-anchor` | Solana hash anchoring service used by the mesh anchor layer |
| `attestto-app` | Mobile PWA wallet — connects to mesh for credential sync and recovery |
